display.purpose: Page titles, composer labels, and major authenticated headings.

headline.purpose: Panel titles and compact section headings.

title.purpose: Rows, student names, and local emphasis.

body.purpose: Evidence text, helper copy, and product UI prose.

label.purpose: Ledger headers, metadata labels, and form labels.

shadows.name: paper
shadows.purpose: Tactile separation for capture composer, evidence cards, and popovers.

shadows.name: floating
shadows.purpose: Rare elevated surfaces that need stronger paper separation.

motion.name: state-transition
motion.purpose: Small state feedback only; no decorative choreography.

motion.name: active-press
motion.purpose: Subtle button press feedback for existing button primitive.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Rust primary action for the one main action in a local area.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.375rem; height: 2.25rem; padding: 0 0.875rem; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 0.5625rem; background: var(--primary, #b85a32); color: var(--primary-foreground, #fbf7ed); font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; box-shadow: var(--shadow-paper, 0 10px 24px rgba(31, 25, 17, 0.12)); transition: background-color 180ms ease, box-shadow 180ms ease, transform 120ms ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--primary, #b85a32) 90%, black); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: var(--ring, #b85a32); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in srgb, var(--ring, #b85a32) 35%, transparent), var(--shadow-paper, 0 10px 24px rgba(31, 25, 17, 0.12)); }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Calm secondary action for navigation, confirmation, and low-drama controls.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.375rem; height: 2rem; padding: 0 0.75rem; border: 1px solid var(--border, #ddd0be); border-radius: 0.5625rem; background: var(--card, #fbf7ed); color: var(--foreground, #182536); font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; transition: background-color 180ms ease, color 180ms ease, border-color 180ms ease, transform 120ms ease; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: var(--muted, #ebe3d6); } .ds-btn-outline:active { transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: var(--ring, #b85a32); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in srgb, var(--ring, #b85a32) 30%, transparent); }

components.name: Paper Card
components.description: Tactile paper panel for composer, evidence, and work surfaces.
components.html:
What happened?

Type a quick note for one student, then review before saving.
components.css: .ds-paper-card { max-width: 360px; padding: 1.25rem; border: 1px solid var(--border, #ddd0be); border-radius: var(--radius-card, 1.125rem); background: var(--card, #fbf7ed); color: var(--foreground, #182536); box-shadow: var(--shadow-paper, 0 10px 24px rgba(31, 25, 17, 0.12)); font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-paper-card h3 { margin: 0 0 0.5rem; font-family: Fraunces, Georgia, serif; font-size: 1.375rem; line-height: 1.2; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -0.02em; } .ds-paper-card p { margin: 0; color: var(--muted-foreground, #5d554a); font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.6; }

components.name: Text Field
components.description: Forgiving paper field for roster forms and review inputs.
components.css: .ds-field { display: grid; gap: 0.375rem; color: var(--foreground, #182536); font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; } .ds-field input { height: 2.5rem; border: 1px solid var(--border, #ddd0be); border-radius: var(--radius, 0.5rem); background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--background, #f3eadc) 50%, transparent); color: var(--foreground, #182536); padding: 0 0.75rem; font: inherit; outline: none; transition: border-color 180ms ease, box-shadow 180ms ease; } .ds-field input::placeholder { color: var(--muted-foreground, #5d554a); } .ds-field input:focus-visible { border-color: var(--ring, #b85a32); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in srgb, var(--ring, #b85a32) 24%, transparent); }

components.name: Muted Chip
components.description: Quiet organizational chip for tags, hints, and metadata.
components.css: .ds-chip-muted { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; min-height: 1.25rem; padding: 0.125rem 0.625rem; border: 1px solid var(--border, #ddd0be); border-radius: 9999px; background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--muted, #ebe3d6) 60%, transparent); color: var(--link, #2c526f); font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; white-space: nowrap; }

components.name: Validated Pill
components.description: Sage validation badge for saved evidence states.
components.css: .ds-validated-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; padding: 0.25rem 0.625rem; border: 1px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--validated, #c7d4a6) 60%, transparent); border-radius: 0.5625rem; background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--validated, #c7d4a6) 35%, transparent); color: var(--validated-foreground, #182536); font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 600; } .ds-validated-pill span { width: 0.5rem; height: 0.5rem; border-radius: 9999px; background: currentColor; }

components.name: Top Navigation Item
components.description: Light top-nav item with rust active state and compact product rhythm.
components.css: .ds-nav-item { position: relative; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; height: 2.75rem; padding: 0 0.75rem; border-radius: 0.5625rem; color: var(--muted-foreground, #5d554a); font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; text-decoration: none; transition: background-color 180ms ease, color 180ms ease; } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--muted, #ebe3d6) 60%, transparent); color: var(--foreground, #182536); } .ds-nav-item:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in srgb, var(--ring, #b85a32) 24%, transparent); } .ds-nav-item-active { color: var(--foreground, #182536); } .ds-nav-item-active::after { content: ""; position: absolute; left: 0.75rem; right: 0.75rem; bottom: -0.75rem; height: 2px; border-radius: 9999px; background: var(--primary, #b85a32); }

narrative.overview:
ClassTrace should feel like a quiet teacher workspace where quick classroom observations become organized evidence without ceremony. The surface is warm paper, not corporate glass; the structure is a desk tray and ledger, not an analytics dashboard. Every visual decision should help a tired teacher capture one student-specific moment, review it calmly, and trust what was saved.

The system is restrained and tactile. Paper-toned surfaces, soft borders, and limited shadow create enough layering to separate capture, review, roster, and timeline work without turning the app into a stack of decorative cards. The rust primary color is intentionally rare: it marks real action, current state, or focus, not decoration.
keyCharacteristics: Capture-first hierarchy with the composer or current work surface near the top.
Warm paper surfaces with ink-forward text and restrained accents.
Ledger-like lists for roster and evidence where scanning matters.
Teacher-controlled validation states that never imply system certainty.
Familiar controls, small transitions, and visible focus states.

rules.name: The Rust Rarity Rule
rules.body: Rust belongs to primary actions, active indicators, and focus states. If a screen has rust everywhere, the hierarchy is broken.

rules.name: The Validation Is Sage Rule
rules.body: Saved or validated evidence uses sage. Do not replace validation with blue info states, gold celebration, or loud success green.

rules.name: The No Rainbow Tags Rule
rules.body: Tags organize evidence; they are not decoration. Use muted, bordered chips unless a scoped component pattern says otherwise.

rules.name: The App Is Not a Poster Rule
rules.body: Hero-scale type belongs to the public landing page only. Authenticated product surfaces use compact, scannable hierarchy.

rules.name: The Labels Stay Sans Rule
rules.body: Buttons, form labels, nav items, metadata, and table-like headers use Inter, never Fraunces or Caveat.

rules.name: The Border-First Rule
rules.body: If a border can explain the surface, use the border. Add shadow only when the surface needs tactile paper separation.

rules.name: The No Ghost-Card Rule
rules.body: Do not pair decorative 1px borders with large generic drop shadows. Shadows here are paper-like and purposeful, not SaaS gloss.

dos: Do keep fast capture visually prominent on evidence surfaces.
Do use semantic tokens from app/globals.css rather than raw colors in components.
Do use rust for primary action, active navigation, and focus hierarchy only.
Do use sage for validated evidence states.
Do make roster and evidence lists ledger-like when scanning matters.
Do keep archive calmer and easier to choose than permanent delete.
Do use plain teacher language: Capture, Evidence feed, What happened?, Review before saving, Student, Tags, Follow-up, Timeline.
Do keep focus rings, labels, errors, and icon-only accessible names visible and reliable.
donts: Don't make ClassTrace look like an enterprise analytics dashboard, SIS, gradebook, compliance factory, gamified classroom app, child-facing product, social feed, generic SaaS template, or AI-powered documentation tool.
Don't add fake controls for workflows that do not exist, including inert search, notifications, reporting dashboards, review queues, or admin tools.
Don't use AI language, compliance overclaims, district approval language, or generated-documentation copy in V1/pre-beta UI.
Don't use bright rainbow tag systems, decorative gradients, glassmorphism, neon effects, heavy shadows, or dashboard chart styling.
Don't use Caveat in authenticated app forms, validation states, dense controls, or critical instructions.
Don't use card grids when a ledger/list is clearer.
Don't create class-scoped capture, classwide note affordances, multi-student capture flows, upload controls, or report navigation without an approved product unit.
